method: 3 rounds x 4 lenses (state-machines, save-persistence, combat-numeric, web-platform), loop-until-dry; every candidate re-traced by an independent verifier instructed to refute. 55 agents total.
note: Entries are raw hunt output, ordered as returned. Independent lenses sometimes found the same bug from different angles - duplicates left in as corroboration (e.g. the WIN_DELAY pause freeze). Verdict REFUTED entries were dropped by the harness and do not appear.

bugs.title: Pausing (or opening dialogue) during the 3s win delay loads the win screen into a paused tree - permanent freeze, win screen lost forever
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:260 (also game.gd:94-107, pause_menu.gd:113-123, win_screen.gd:9)
bugs.scenario: Kill Irene, then press Esc within the 3s WIN_DELAY (natural: 'let me Save Now'). pause_menu sets get_tree().paused = true. The SceneTreeTimer from get_tree().create_timer(WIN_DELAY) has process_always=true, so it fires while paused and resumes _on_boss_defeated -> Game.change_scene(WIN_SCENE). Game is PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS so its fade tween and change_scene_to_file run while paused; main.tscn (containing the only PauseMenu) is freed, but get_tree().paused stays true. win_screen.tscn's root Control is PAUSABLE, so its _unhandled_input (win_screen.gd:9) never runs and no surviving node can unpause. Alternate trigger: within the 3s, step to the library-door talker at ~(162,17) (~140px from the boss, reachable at 60px/s) and press E - DialogueBox.open() pauses the tree the same way, and additionally leaks Game.dialogue_active=true when its scene is freed.
bugs.impact: Hard soft-lock on the quest-complete screen: no input works, only a page reload escapes. Because boss_irene.gd:147-148 already saved boss_defeated=true, after reload the boss is cleared and the win screen can never be triggered again - the ending is permanently swallowed.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully traced and confirmed. (1) boss_irene.gd:147-150 saves boss_defeated=true then emits defeated; encounter_manager.gd:259-261 sets win_pending and awaits get_tree().create_timer(3.0) — Godot's create_timer defaults process_always=true, so it fires while paused. (2) pause_menu.gd:94-100 gates Esc only on dialogue_active, not win_pending (win_pending gates only level_transition.gd), so Esc within the 3s window sets get_tree().paused=true (line 121); the interactable/dialogue path (interactable.gd:49, dialogue_box.gd:47-48) is equally ungated and pauses identically, plus leaks dialogue_active=true when main.tscn is freed. (3) The timer's signal resumes the coroutine despite the pause; Game is PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS (game.gd:71) so its pause-bound fade tween and change_scene_to_file run while paused, freeing main.tscn — which contains all three of the codebase's only paused=false sites (pause_menu.gd:128,175; dialogue_box.gd:74; verified by grep). (4) SceneTree.paused persists across the swap; win_screen.tscn root has no process_mode override (INHERIT→pausable), so win_screen.gd:9 _unhandled_input never fires, and the sole autoload Game (project.godot:20) has no input handler — hard soft-lock, only a reload escapes. (5) After reload+Continue, _apply_resume (encounter_manager.gd:186-187) clears the boss and WIN_SCENE is referenced only in encounter_manager, so the saved run's ending is unreachable. Sole overstatement: it's not literally permanent — New Game (player_select.gd:119 reset_run) clears boss_defeated, so the ending is recoverable by a full replay. Fix direction: gate pause/dialogue on win_pending, or clear paused in Game.change_scene.

bugs.title: Scene change during WIN_DELAY kills the awaiting coroutine - win screen never shows and Game.win_pending is stuck true, disabling every door/edge for the session
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:256-261 (also pause_menu.gd:174-176, game.gd:94-96, level_transition.gd:45,66)
bugs.scenario: Trigger (a), solo: kill Irene, press Esc, choose 'Return to Title' inside the 3s window - _return_to_title is not gated on win_pending. Game.change_scene(player_select) frees the valley scene including EncounterManager while _on_boss_defeated is suspended at the await; when the timer fires, Godot cannot resume a function state of a freed instance, so Game.change_scene(WIN_SCENE) never runs. Trigger (b), co-op: P2 steps on any door in the ~0.2s before the killing blow - the door's fade is already in flight when the boss dies, the swap frees EncounterManager mid-await (or if the timer fires during the fade, change_scene returns early at game.gd:96 because _fading is true). Either way win_pending, set at line 259, is never cleared: reset_run() (game.gd:118-124) only runs from the win screen or New Game.
bugs.impact: The quest-complete screen is silently swallowed (boss_defeated is already saved, so it can never be re-earned), and with win_pending stuck true every LevelTransition refuses travel (level_transition.gd:45,66) for the rest of the session - in trigger (b) the party is trapped inside the interior they entered, and even after Continue all valley doors are dead. Only New Game or an app restart recovers.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced every cited line; the claim holds. encounter_manager.gd:256-261 sets Game.win_pending=true then suspends 3s on `await get_tree().create_timer(WIN_DELAY).timeout` with `Game.change_scene(WIN_SCENE)` after the await; EncounterManager lives inside main.tscn ($World/EncounterManager, main.gd:199), so any scene change during the window frees it and Godot aborts the resume of a freed instance's suspended function — WIN_SCENE never loads. Trigger (a) is real: pause_menu.gd:174-176 `_return_to_title()` has no win_pending gate and game.gd:94-96 change_scene only checks _fading, so Esc → Return to Title inside the 3s window (timer is process_always, keeps ticking while paused) frees the manager mid-await. Trigger (b) co-op door race also traces: _travel() runs pre-kill with win_pending false, the 0.22s FADE_HALF swap frees the manager mid-await (the claim's alternative "timer fires during fade → early return at game.gd:96" sub-path is effectively unreachable since 3.0s >> ~0.44s fade cycle, but the primary freed-coroutine mechanism always wins). Impact confirmed: boss_irene.gd:147-148 saves boss_defeated=true BEFORE emitting defeated, and encounter_manager.gd:186-187 clears the boss area on any re-entry, so defeat can never re-fire on that save; win_pending is cleared only by reset_run() (game.gd:123), reachable only from win_screen.gd:11 (never shows) or player_select New Game (player_select.gd:119) — Continue's load_state() never touches it. With win_pending stuck true, level_transition.gd:45 and :66 refuse all door/edge travel for the session; in trigger (b) the party is trapped in the interior. Real functional bug reachable via normal UI, not cosmetic. Suggested fixes: gate _return_to_title on win_pending (or have it go straight to WIN_SCENE), and/or move the win timer to the Game autoload so a scene swap can't kill it, and/or clear win_pending in change_scene/load_state as a belt-and-braces reset.

bugs.title: Boss never re-activates when the valley is re-entered through a door/edge with checkpoint already at BOSS_ID - inert Irene can be killed for a free, silent win
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:216-222 (also encounter_manager.gd:196-199, level_transition.gd:73, enemy.gd:90-98, boss_irene.gd:53-66)
bugs.scenario: The boss fight starts when checkpoint reaches 4 (_update_checkpoint -> _activate_area). The library door transition (161-163,17) sits inside the arena: mid-fight, run 9 tiles north into the library, then step back out. _travel set resume_requested=false, so on the valley reload _apply_resume returns at line 189 without activating her, and _update_checkpoint only calls _activate_area when reached > Game.checkpoint - impossible once checkpoint==4. No other code path calls activate(). A fresh BossIrene spawns with _active=false: she stands still, throws nothing, shows no HP bar (boss_irene.gd:62-66), but Enemy.take_damage is not gated on _active, so sword/arrow hits land, taunt thresholds are skipped, and _die() fires normally. Same result via a cove round trip, or Save Now in the cove then Continue and walk back west.
bugs.impact: The final boss becomes a defenseless target dummy: free kill, boss_defeated saved, win screen granted with zero fight. Any player who ducks into the library door to escape the fight (an obvious panic move) hits this.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced end-to-end; every link holds. (1) Checkpoint hits BOSS_ID=4 before the library door is reachable: boss area center is cell (156,26) (encounter_manager.gd:149) and _update_checkpoint is x-progress based, while the library door trigger Rect2i(161,17,3,1) (level_registry.gd:72) lies east of x=156 — so anyone at the door has checkpoint==4 autosaved. (2) LevelTransition._travel (level_transition.gd:63-74) has no boss-fight gate (only win_pending/fade) and sets Game.resume_requested=false at line 73. (3) On valley re-entry, Game (autoload) keeps checkpoint=4/boss_defeated=false; setup() respawns a fresh BossIrene with _active=false, and _apply_resume returns at encounter_manager.gd:189 before the line-198 activation. (4) Grep confirms activate() is only called via _activate_area, whose three call sites are all dead here: _apply_resume:199 needs resume_requested; _update_checkpoint:216-222 needs reached>checkpoint, impossible since 4 is the max area id; _rearm_area:301 needs a full wipe, which won't happen — the inactive boss deals zero damage (her _physics_process override early-returns and never calls _try_contact_damage even when active). (5) Player sword hits anything with take_damage (player.gd:364-369); Enemy.take_damage (enemy.gd:90-98) gates only on _dead, BossIrene doesn't override it; taunts/phase-2/HP-bar are behind the _active gate (boss_irene.gd:62-66), so an inert Irene is silently killable and _die() (boss_irene.gd:143-154) sets boss_defeated, saves, and emits defeated -> win screen. Alternate routes verified too: cove east-edge crossing (Rect2i(190,24,2,5)) also clears resume_requested, and cove Save Now + Continue resumes in the peaceful cove (setup returns early on empty areas) followed by an ordinary west crossing. Minor claim inaccuracies that don't affect the verdict: the door is ~9 rows north of the boss row but the from_library arrival cell (162,18) proves the path is walkable; also on such re-entries ALL valley areas respawn (the prior-area clear loop is behind the same resume_requested return), a related symptom of the same root cause. Real, non-cosmetic exploit: free silent boss kill with a legitimate win saved.

bugs.title: Big slime has no aggro gate and marches off its post from scene load, unraveling the area-3 road encounter
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scenes\enemy_slime_big.tscn:16-21 (no aggro_radius override; enemy.gd:157-162, encounter_manager.gd:140-147)
bugs.scenario: enemy_slime_big.tscn never sets aggro_radius, so it keeps enemy.gd's default 0.0 and _awake() returns true from spawn with no range cap on _nearest_player(). From the moment the valley loads, the tank slime at cell (138,26) walks west at 16 px/s toward the players in the village ~1500px away, steering around props. Its escort bats (aggro 90) and every other area 2/3 enemy are ambush-gated, and the area comment says it should sit 'astride the road' as a pacing beat. After a few minutes of play it arrives mid-camp-fight or is met wandering the forest; on a wipe only the checkpoint area is rearmed (encounter_manager.gd:236-239), so return_home never resets it until checkpoint 3 is actually recorded.
bugs.impact: The mid-road gate encounter never happens as designed; instead a 10-HP, 2-damage tank joins earlier fights (notably the already-dense forest camp) uninvited, and can harass checkpoint respawns it was never meant to reach.
bugs.verifier_note: Verified line-by-line. enemy_slime_big.tscn (lines 12-28) sets no aggro_radius, so the big slime keeps enemy.gd's default 0.0; _awake() (enemy.gd:157-162) then returns true unconditionally and _nearest_player() (165-177) has no range cap, so from the first physics frame after the valley loads it walks at 16 px/s toward the nearest player with steer-around-obstacle behavior (steer_duration default 0.35 applies). It spawns at cell (138,26) = world (2216,424), ~2100px from the "S" spawn (cell (6,25)) and ~1490px from the village slime fight — the claim's ~1500px figure matches. Every other mobile enemy in areas 1-3 is ambush-gated (bat 90, skeleton 110, bowman 130, mage 140; bombschroom is stationary with velocity=ZERO), and both the encounter comment ("a tank slime astride the road", encounter_manager.gd:136-137) and enemy.gd's own aggro_radius doc ("wakes when you enter the clearing instead of marching at you from level load") prove the intent it violates. The wipe claim also holds: _on_downed (encounter_manager.gd:231-239) rearms only the current checkpoint's area, and checkpoint 3 is only recorded when a live player passes x=2120 (cell 132), so return_home() never reaches the wandering slime during areas 0-2. No global gating exists (no VisibleOnScreenEnabler, no activate() for non-boss enemies; tree pauses only during dialogue/menus). Only unprovable detail is whether terrain lets it reach the village vs. snagging in the forest, but the claim hedges exactly that, and either way the mid-road encounter is unraveled — the tank departs at load while its gated bat escorts stay home. Real gameplay bug, not cosmetic. Fix is a one-line aggro_radius override in enemy_slime_big.tscn (~100-120 to match its escort/peers).

bugs.title: Wipe during WIN_DELAY at the boss checkpoint resurrects a full-HP, activated Irene who attacks the respawned players until the win screen interrupts
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:286-301 (also encounter_manager.gd:231-239, boss_irene.gd:143-154)
bugs.scenario: The killing blow lands while a thrown book or phase-2 bat is about to finish the last standing player. Boss dies at t=0 (win_pending=true, 3s timer starts, defeat line shows, corpse fades over 3.5s). Player hits 0 HP at ~t=0.2, downed.emit() fires after the 0.7s collapse -> _on_downed -> all players down -> respawn at checkpoint 4 and _rearm_area(boss area). The area is not 'cleared' because the fading boss corpse is still a valid instance (boss_irene.gd:151-154 frees it only ~3.5s post-death), so the BOSS_ID branch frees the corpse mid-defeat-line, spawns a brand-new full-HP Irene, and _activate_area makes her deliver her intro line and start throwing books at the players who just respawned 6 tiles away. ~2s later the win-delay timer fires and the win screen loads anyway.
bugs.impact: After winning, the player watches the defeat line get cut off, the boss resurrect and attack them (they can even be downed a second time, spawning yet another boss), then the quest-complete screen pops mid-chaos. boss_defeated was already saved so state recovers after the win screen, but the ending sequence is badly broken whenever the mutual-kill race lands.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully traced and confirmed. The chain holds link by link: (1) BossIrene._die (boss_irene.gd:143-154) keeps the corpse a valid instance for ~3.5s (fade tween 1.2s + 2.3s interval before queue_free; her _physics_process early-returns on _dead so Enemy's 0.6s death-free path never runs), so EncounterManager._process never marks the boss area cleared during the 3.0s WIN_DELAY. (2) Game.win_pending gates ONLY doors (level_transition.gd:45,66) — not player damage, not enemy AI, not _on_downed. (3) The player can genuinely die post-victory: in-flight books live 4s and hit players (book_projectile.gd:33-36), and phase-2 bats in "boss_summons" are freed by nothing except _rearm_area — neither the boss's _die nor _on_boss_defeated touches them, so they keep attacking through the win delay. (4) Lethal hit at t~0.2 -> downed.emit() at t~0.9 (DEATH_DURATION 0.7, player.gd:258-260) -> _on_downed (encounter_manager.gd:231-239, no win gate) -> checkpoint is necessarily BOSS_ID during the fight -> _rearm_area's BOSS_ID branch (286-301) frees the fading corpse (killing the defeat line, a child Label of the boss node), spawns a fresh full-HP Irene 6 cells from the respawn point, and _activate_area triggers her intro line and 1.7s throw timer (first book at ~t=2.6). (5) The awaited SceneTree timer still fires at t=3.0 and loads the win screen mid-fight; boss_defeated was saved at death so _apply_resume (186-187) recovers state afterward. One embellishment is wrong: "downed a second time, spawning yet another boss" is arithmetically unreachable — 6 HP, 1-damage hits, 0.6s invuln per hit plus 0.6s respawn invuln and 0.7s collapse needs ~4.3s, but only ~2.1s remain before the win screen. That does not affect the core finding. Real functional bug in the ending sequence, reachable whenever the player is downed during WIN_DELAY at the boss checkpoint; fix would be gating _on_downed/_rearm_area on Game.win_pending (or treating Game.boss_defeated as cleared in _rearm_area).

bugs.title: Checkpoint wipe only rearms the checkpoint's own area - aggro-latched enemies from the next area (bats faster than the player) chase down and camp the respawn point
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:236-239 (also enemy.gd:157-162, enemy_bat.tscn:18,22)
bugs.scenario: Checkpoint 2 records at x=1736 (cell 108). Area 3's western bat sits at cell 135 (x=2168) with aggro_radius 90, so it latches awake once a player reaches x>=2078 - 42px before the area-3 checkpoint line at x=2120 ever records (checkpoint is x-of-area-center based, encounter_manager.gd:205-217). Party pushes east out of the camp fight, wakes the bats, and wipes. _on_downed rearms only _area_by_id(Game.checkpoint) = area 2; area 3's survivors are never sent home and the aggro latch is permanent. The bats (move_speed 62 vs player 60) fly the ~400px west and re-engage the players at the checkpoint, who have only 0.6s of respawn invulnerability; the ungated big slime compounds it.
bugs.impact: Respawn camping: retries at the camp checkpoint begin with off-post area-3 enemies already inbound or on top of the players, contradicting the documented rearm contract ('survivors just reset to their post and idle again') and enabling a repeat-wipe loop for weaker players.
bugs.verifier_note: All cited numbers verified against source. map_data.gd cell_center = cell*16+8: area-2 checkpoint x=1736, area-3 checkpoint x=2120, area-3 western bat at (2168,408) with aggro_radius 90 (enemy_bat.tscn:22) latches at player x≈2079 on the road — ~41px before checkpoint 3 can record (_update_checkpoint, encounter_manager.gd:213-214), and the camp's own east bat at x=2024 naturally pulls fights into that window. The latch is permanent with no leash (enemy.gd:157-162), and _on_downed (encounter_manager.gd:236-239) calls _rearm_area only on _area_by_id(Game.checkpoint); return_home (the only thing that clears _aggro) is never called on neighboring areas. Latched bats (move_speed 62 vs player max_speed 60, player.gd:78) chase the respawned players ~400px west in ~6-7s versus 0.6s respawn invulnerability (player.gd:70,353) and cannot be outwalked. Big slime confirmed ungated (no aggro_radius in enemy_slime_big.tscn → default 0 = always awake). Two minor caveats: the doc comment literally promises rearming only "the area" (so it defeats the stated checkpoint-placement intent at encounter_manager.gd:102-104 more than a written contract), and bats are 1 HP so the repeat-wipe loop threatens weak players rather than guaranteeing one. The respawn-camping mechanism itself is provably real.

bugs.title: Esc during a scene-change fade carries get_tree().paused into the next scene - next level loads frozen; the title screen variant is a hard lock
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:94-107 (also pause_menu.gd:91-123, pause_menu.gd:174-176)
bugs.scenario: Game.change_scene has a 0.22s fade-out during which the old scene still runs and pause_menu._unhandled_input has no is_fading gate. Walk into any door, press Esc within the 0.22s window: the menu opens and sets paused=true; Game (PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS) finishes the fade and swaps scenes anyway; the open PauseMenu is freed with the old scene and paused is never cleared, so the destination level loads fully frozen mid-fade-in (recoverable only by guessing Esc -> Resume on the new scene's fresh menu). Worse variant: after 'Return to Title' (which leaves _open=true and the panel visible), pressing Esc twice during its fade re-pauses the tree just before player_select loads - player_select is PAUSABLE with no pause menu, so the title screen accepts no input at all.
bugs.impact: Door crossings appear to hard-freeze the game to anyone who pauses at the wrong instant (confusing but recoverable via a blind Esc); the double-Esc-on-quit variant soft-locks the title screen until the page is reloaded.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully traced and confirmed. game.gd:94-107 fades 0.22s (FADE_HALF, game.gd:21) with the old scene running unpaused; pause_menu.gd:91-100 gates Esc only on _controls_open and Game.dialogue_active — no is_fading() gate (grep: is_fading is checked only in level_transition.gd). Esc in the window sets get_tree().paused=true (pause_menu.gd:121); Game is PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS (game.gd:71) so its bound tween keeps running while paused, the await resumes, and change_scene_to_file (game.gd:101) swaps with paused still true. pause_menu.tscn is instanced only in main.tscn, has no _exit_tree cleanup, and nothing else ever clears SceneTree.paused (only pause_menu.gd and dialogue_box.gd write it; main.gd never does) — so the destination level loads frozen. Recoverable: the new main.tscn's fresh ALWAYS-mode PauseMenu opens visibly on Esc ("blind Esc" is slightly overstated). Title variant confirmed as a genuine soft lock: _return_to_title (pause_menu.gd:174-176) unpauses but leaves _open=true/panel visible; two Esc presses during its 0.22s fade re-pause (close no-op, then re-open sets paused=true), and player_select.tscn has no process_mode override (only pause_menu/controls_menu set process_mode=3 in any .tscn) and no pause menu — a PAUSABLE Control in a paused tree receives no _unhandled_input or gui_input, so keyboard and mouse are dead until reload/restart. Caveat on severity: both need Esc within the 0.22s race window (title lock needs two presses in it), so it's an edge-timing bug, but the mechanism and impacts are exactly as claimed.

bugs.title: Dying or getting hurt during the sword swing's active frames leaves the white swing arc frozen on screen (indefinitely for a co-op downed player)
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\player.gd:426-443 (also player.gd:329-345)
bugs.scenario: During ATTACK's active window (0.1-0.28s) _process_attack calls queue_redraw every frame, so the CanvasItem's last draw is a visible arc segment. If take_damage lands a fatal (or non-fatal) hit in that window, _enter_down/_enter_hurt switch state but never call queue_redraw, and neither do _process_down/_process_hurt - the canvas retains the last-drawn arc. _draw's early-return guard only takes effect on the next redraw, which doesn't happen until _enter_normal (player.gd:281). Solo: the arc floats over the corpse for the full 0.7s death animation plus the respawn transition. Co-op: a downed player waiting for a clear-revive shows the frozen arc for however long the teammate takes - potentially minutes.
bugs.impact: Cosmetic but clearly visible glitch: a stale white slash effect hovers over a collapsed/hurt character, following the body, until the player next returns to NORMAL state.
bugs.verifier_note: Real but cosmetic, and the trigger is narrower than claimed. Verified in scripts/player.gd: the only queue_redraw calls are line 201 (_process_attack) and line 281 (_enter_normal); _enter_hurt (329-337), _enter_down (339-345), _process_hurt, and _process_down never redraw, and Godot CanvasItem draw commands persist until the next queue_redraw — so a last-drawn arc does freeze, following the body. However, frame ordering splits the damage sources: (1) Melee contact and bomb blasts (enemy.gd:180-187, bomb_enemy.gd:69-76) run in enemy _physics_process, and enemies are added to $World after the tscn-declared players (encounter_manager.gd:169-177), so they hit AFTER the player's _process_attack already queued a redraw that tick — the pending redraw services with state HURT/DOWN, _draw's guard early-returns, and the arc is CLEARED, not frozen. The most common damage source does not reproduce the bug. (2) Projectile hits (enemy_bolt.gd:32-35 — bowman/mage/boss; book_projectile.gd:33-36 — boss) arrive via Area2D body_entered, which Godot 4 flushes at the top of a physics tick BEFORE all _physics_process callbacks; state flips to HURT/DOWN with no redraw ever queued afterward, so the previous frame's arc freezes exactly as claimed. Durations check out: HURT ~0.25s (trivial), solo death 0.7s of arc floating over the corpse, co-op downed player indefinitely until _update_coop_revive (encounter_manager.gd:242-253) — nothing hides the Player canvas item while down. Purely visual, no gameplay effect; fix is a queue_redraw() in _enter_hurt/_enter_down (or in the state-exit path).

bugs.title: Refresh after killing the boss (during the 3s win delay or on the win screen) permanently locks the save out of ever completing the quest
bugs.where: scripts/boss_irene.gd:147-148; scripts/encounter_manager.gd:186-187, 256-261; scripts/win_screen.gd:9-13
bugs.scenario: 1) Kill Irene. boss_irene._die() immediately sets Game.boss_defeated = true and calls Game.save() (boss_irene.gd:147-148). 2) Close/refresh the browser within the 3-second WIN_DELAY (encounter_manager.gd:260), or on the win screen before pressing accept. 3) Reopen, choose Continue. _apply_resume sees boss_defeated and clears the boss area (encounter_manager.gd:186-187), so the boss never exists to emit `defeated` again; the win screen is only ever loaded from _on_boss_defeated, and the run is only reset by the win screen's accept handler (win_screen.gd:11-12), which is now unreachable.
bugs.impact: A player who legitimately beat the game but refreshed at the wrong moment gets a save where the quest can never complete: no win screen, boss gone, checkpoint stuck at 4, forever. Only escape is New Game. The save file itself is the carrier — boss_defeated=true is persisted before the win screen is guaranteed to show.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully verified by tracing the code. boss_irene.gd:147-148 persists boss_defeated=true (with checkpoint already at 4) synchronously at the kill, via Game.save() -> game.gd:147 (localStorage on web / user:// JSON on desktop; restored by game.gd:198). The win screen only appears after a 3.0s awaited timer (encounter_manager.gd:256-261, WIN_DELAY at line 37), and the run is only reset by a ui_accept press on the win screen (win_screen.gd:9-13). A refresh/close anywhere in that window — including at the win screen itself, the natural "beat the game, close tab" moment — leaves the save poisoned. On Continue, player_select.gd:113-114 restores the flag, and _apply_resume (encounter_manager.gd:186-187) queue_frees the boss and marks her area cleared before _activate_area runs on the now-empty instance list, so `defeated` can never be emitted again. Grep across scripts/ confirms no alternate path: WIN_SCENE is loaded only from _on_boss_defeated, and reset_run() is called only from the (unreachable) win screen and player_select's New Game. The save can never complete the quest; only New Game escapes. Genuine functional dead-end, not cosmetic — mitigated only in that the player has already seen the boss die, so the lost content is the quest-complete screen and auto-reset rather than gameplay. Fix suggestion: have _apply_resume (or main) route straight to the win screen when boss_defeated is true and the run was never reset, or defer the boss_defeated save until the win screen's accept handler.
bugs.resolution: FIXED in PR #24 (merged 2026-07-17), taking the verifier's own suggestion: the premature Game.boss_defeated=true; Game.save() was REMOVED from boss_irene.gd._die() and deferred to Game.begin_win_sequence() on the always-alive Game autoload, set+saved together AFTER the WIN_DELAY await and just before change_scene(WIN_SCENE). A refresh/close during the delay now leaves boss_defeated=false on disk; on Continue the boss respawns awake (via _apply_resume's persisted checkpoint>=BOSS_ID re-wake) and the ending is re-earnable. Note: the hunt's boss_irene.gd:147-148 ref was already stale (the save sat at 165-166 in current source pre-fix), and post-fix that save no longer exists in boss_irene.gd at all. Verified: gdformat --check + gdlint clean, all five stdlib checkers green; the native Godot force-quit-mid-delay playtest is still owed on Windows.

bugs.title: Pausing during the 3s win delay loads the win screen into a paused tree with no way to unpause — hard freeze requiring a refresh
bugs.where: scripts/encounter_manager.gd:260-261; scripts/pause_menu.gd:113-128; scripts/win_screen.gd:9-13
bugs.scenario: 1) Kill Irene, then press Esc during her 3-second defeat line (pause menu opens, get_tree().paused = true). 2) The win timer is a SceneTree.create_timer with default process_always = true, so it keeps ticking while paused; Game (PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS) executes change_scene to the win screen while the tree is still paused. 3) The pause menu — the only node that ever sets paused = false — is freed with the old main scene; SceneTree.paused survives the scene change. 4) win_screen is default PAUSABLE, so its _unhandled_input is never delivered: ui_accept does nothing.
bugs.impact: Frozen win screen, zero responsive input, only a page refresh recovers — and because boss_defeated was already saved, the refresh lands in the previous bug: the quest-complete screen is then permanently unreachable on that save. Two end-of-game bugs compound at the single most likely moment for a player to pause (the victory line).
bugs.verifier_note: Traced end-to-end in code; every step holds. (1) Esc works during the 3s win delay: Irene's defeat line is a boss-local floating Label (boss_irene.gd:130-133), not the dialogue box, so Game.dialogue_active is false and pause_menu.gd:97 doesn't block; PauseMenu is PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS (pause_menu.tscn:24) and sets get_tree().paused = true (pause_menu.gd:121). (2) encounter_manager.gd:260 uses get_tree().create_timer(3.0) whose Godot-4 default process_always=true ticks through pause, and signal delivery isn't pause-gated, so the await resumes and calls Game.change_scene while paused. (3) Game is PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS (game.gd:71) so its fade tween (TWEEN_PAUSE_BOUND) runs and change_scene_to_file executes (game.gd:101); nothing in game.gd touches paused, and SceneTree.paused survives scene swaps. (4) The only three `paused = false` writers in the repo (dialogue_box.gd:74, pause_menu.gd:128, pause_menu.gd:175) are all inside the freed main scene; win_screen.tscn has no process_mode override, so its root is pausable, frozen, and never receives _unhandled_input — ui_accept is dead. Hard freeze; only a refresh recovers. Compounding half also checks out: boss_irene.gd:147-148 saves boss_defeated=true before the delay, and on Continue encounter_manager.gd:186-187 clears the boss area so `defeated` can never re-fire — the quest-complete screen is permanently unreachable on that save (reset_run only runs from the win screen or New Game). Only escape: player un-pauses within the 3s window before the timer fires (or within the 0.22s fade after); a player who stays in the menu is deterministically frozen. Verified by code trace against documented Godot 4 semantics (timer process_always default, pause gating input callbacks, paused persisting across scene changes); not exercised in a live run.

bugs.title: One key remap permanently strips the legacy alias bindings from all 16 actions after the next boot
bugs.where: scripts/controls.gd:106-119 (_save persists only the first key of every action) and scripts/controls.gd:92-101 (apply_saved erases each action's full event list)
bugs.scenario: 1) project.godot gives several actions two bindings: p1_attack = G + Space, p1_fire = H + F, p2_attack = numpad key + "/", p2_fire = numpad + ",", p2_action2 = numpad + ".". 2) Player remaps ANY single action (say P2 LEFT) in the controls menu. Controls._save() snapshots ALL actions but only their FIRST event each, writing a 16-entry controls.json that omits every alias. 3) In-session everything still works (only the remapped action was erased), so the loss is invisible. 4) Next boot, Controls.apply_saved() erases every listed action's events and installs the single saved key.
bugs.impact: Behavior silently diverges between the remap session and every later session: Space no longer swings P1's sword, F no longer fires the bow, and P2 loses "/", ",", "." — leaving p2_attack/p2_fire/p2_action2 bound only to numpad keys. On a tenkeyless/laptop keyboard, player 2 can no longer attack at all after an unrelated remap, and the controls menu shows nothing wrong (it always displayed only the first binding).
bugs.verifier_note: Fully traced. project.godot gives 5 actions dual bindings (p1_attack G+Space, p1_fire H+F, p2_attack KP1+"/", p2_fire KP2+",", p2_action2 KP3+"."; 4194439-41 are Godot KEY_KP_1..3). Controls.rebind (controls.gd:65-70) erases only the remapped action then calls _save(); _save (controls.gd:106-119) loops over ALL 16 ACTIONS with no changed-only filter and stores only the first InputEventKey per action (break at line 115) into a {action:int} JSON that cannot represent a second binding. In-session nothing breaks (other actions' live events untouched), but next boot game.gd:69 calls apply_saved() (controls.gd:92-101) which action_erase_events + installs the single saved key for every listed action — permanently stripping Space/F/"/",",","." aliases. Gameplay reads these actions (player.gd:174-180; character_creator.gd:106-108 and player_select.gd:86-88 use p1/p2_attack as confirm), so P2 combat becomes numpad-only — unusable on tenkeyless/laptop keyboards. The menu's key_text (controls.gd:53-60) always showed only the first binding, so the loss is invisible; only reset() recovers. Header docs (controls.gd:6-10) say a remap replaces that action's bindings with boot defaults otherwise, so degrading 15 untouched actions is unintended — a real, non-cosmetic silent-divergence bug.

bugs.title: Return to Title during the win delay can drop the win scene change, leaving win_pending stuck true — every door and map edge refuses travel for the rest of the session
bugs.where: scripts/game.gd:94-96 (re-entrant change_scene silently dropped); scripts/encounter_manager.gd:259-261; scripts/level_transition.gd:45,66; scripts/game.gd:118-124 (only reset_run clears win_pending)
bugs.scenario: 1) Kill Irene, quickly Esc → Return to Title in the last ~0.5s of the 3s delay (the timer runs during pause). 2) _return_to_title starts Game.change_scene(player_select) — _fading becomes true for ~0.44s plus scene load. 3) The win timer fires inside that window; _on_boss_defeated calls Game.change_scene(WIN_SCENE), which hits the `if _fading: return` guard and is silently discarded. 4) Game.win_pending remains true (only reset_run or a successful win-screen load path clears it). 5) Player picks Continue: boss_defeated=true resumes fine, but LevelTransition._travel and its retry poll both refuse while win_pending (level_transition.gd:45,66).
bugs.impact: All travel volumes in the game are dead for the rest of the session — the player can walk the valley but cannot enter any building or reach the cove; no error is shown. Recovery requires New Game (wiping the run) or a full page refresh.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced end-to-end in code; every cited line checks out. encounter_manager.gd:259-261 sets Game.win_pending=true then awaits a 3.0s SceneTreeTimer (process_always defaults true, so it runs while the pause menu holds the tree paused). pause_menu.gd blocks Esc only during dialogue_active (no win_pending check), and _return_to_title (lines 174-176) calls Game.change_scene(player_select) with no reset_run. If the win timer fires during the ~0.22s fade-out, Game.change_scene(WIN_SCENE) hits the `if _fading: return` guard (game.gd:95-96) and is silently dropped — exactly as claimed. The bug is actually WIDER than claimed: Return to Title at ANY point in the 3s delay strands the flag, because once change_scene_to_file swaps the scene the scene-local EncounterManager (Game is the sole autoload, project.godot:20) is freed and the awaited continuation never resumes, so change_scene(WIN_SCENE) never runs at all. win_pending=false exists only in reset_run (game.gd:123), reachable only via New Game (player_select.gd:119) or the now-unreachable win screen accept (win_screen.gd:11); it is not persisted, so Continue's load_state doesn't touch it, and boss_defeated=true clears the boss area on resume (encounter_manager.gd:186-187) so the defeat signal can never re-fire. LevelTransition is the sole travel mechanism (doors AND map edges); both _travel (line 66) and the retry poll (line 45) silently refuse while win_pending — all travel dead for the session, no error shown. Recovery only via New Game or app/page restart. Static trace, not runtime-reproduced, but the last-0.22s path needs no engine subtlety and the wider path rests on standard SceneTreeTimer/freed-instance semantics.

bugs.title: Failed saves are reported as successful: last_saved is set before the write is attempted, and both write paths swallow failure
bugs.where: scripts/game.gd:152 (last_saved set unconditionally), 153-154 (web eval swallows localStorage exceptions), 156-159 (desktop silently skips when FileAccess.open returns null); scripts/encounter_manager.gd:216-220 (toast shown regardless)
bugs.scenario: On web: localStorage is full (quota) or blocked (privacy mode / cookies disabled) — localStorage.setItem throws inside JavaScriptBridge.eval, which Godot catches, logs to the browser console, and returns null; save() has no idea. On desktop: FileAccess.open(SAVE_PATH, WRITE) returns null (locked/read-only user dir) and the write is skipped. In both cases last_saved was already set at line 152, so the pause menu shows "Saved HH:MM" and the checkpoint autosave path shows the "checkpoint saved" HUD toast.
bugs.impact: A player on a storage-blocked browser plays an entire session with the UI repeatedly confirming saves, then loses everything on refresh — the exact fail-silent behavior the project's own conventions forbid. has_save() then finds nothing and the title screen quietly skips the Continue option.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced every cited line and all hold. scripts/game.gd:152 sets last_saved before any write attempt. Line 154 (web) calls JavaScriptBridge.eval("localStorage.setItem(...)") discarding the return value; Godot's web eval wraps execution in try/catch and logs exceptions to the browser console returning null, so QuotaExceededError/SecurityError from blocked or full localStorage never reaches GDScript — and save() returns void, so no caller could check anyway. Lines 156-159 (desktop) guard the write with "if file != null" and have no else branch: a null FileAccess.open (locked/read-only user dir) silently skips the save. Downstream UI confirms success regardless: scripts/pause_menu.gd:170-171 shows "Saved HH:MM" from the pre-set last_saved, and scripts/encounter_manager.gd:218-220 shows the checkpoint-saved HUD toast unconditionally after Game.save(). Impact verified too: after a refresh with nothing persisted, _read_raw() returns "" (game.gd:180-182), has_save() returns false, and player_select.gd:42 skips the Continue/New Game stage with no explanation. Real fail-silent data-loss bug, not cosmetic. Fix direction: make save() return an error/bool (check localStorage.setItem success via an eval that returns a sentinel on catch; push_error + return on null FileAccess), only set last_saved on success, and have callers gate the toast/status text on the result.

bugs.title: Save with checkpoint above the max area id (hand-edited or from a future build with fewer areas) clears the boss area and soft-locks the run
bugs.where: scripts/game.gd:196 (checkpoint loaded without clamping); scripts/encounter_manager.gd:192-199 (prior-clear loop) and 326-330 (_area_by_id fallback to _areas[0])
bugs.scenario: 1) Edit the save (localStorage key norfolk_path or user://save.json) to v:4, checkpoint:7 — _apply accepts it verbatim, no clamp against any max. 2) Continue. _apply_resume: _area_by_id(7) misses and falls back to _areas[0], spawning the party back at the village; the prior-clear loop `prior.id < 7` then clears EVERY area including the boss (Irene is freed, area marked cleared, boss_defeated still false). 3) _activate_area on the boss area is a no-op (instances empty). 4) _update_checkpoint is monotonic, so checkpoint 7 is never corrected and re-saves keep it.
bugs.impact: An empty valley with an unkillable (nonexistent) boss: the win is unreachable and the broken checkpoint persists across every Continue. Also inconsistent: players spawn at area 0 while all beacons show lit. A one-line clamp of checkpoint to the known area range at load or in _apply_resume would fix it. Requires a tampered/foreign save, so low likelihood but total loss when hit.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully traced and reproduces as described. game.gd:196 loads checkpoint verbatim with no upper clamp (player_count on the next line IS clamped, checkpoint is not); has_save/_apply accept any v3/v4 dict, so a save with checkpoint:7 is offered as Continue and applied. In _apply_resume (encounter_manager.gd:182-199): _area_by_id(7) misses all ids 0-4 and falls back to _areas[0] (lines 326-330, the village), so players spawn at area 0; the prior-clear loop (prior.id < 7) clears EVERY area including boss id 4 — _clear_area queue_frees Irene and marks the area cleared while boss_defeated stays false (it is only set in boss_irene.gd:147 on an actual kill; queue_free never emits `defeated`). _activate_area on the boss area then iterates an empty instances array (no-op). _rearm_area early-returns on cleared areas so the boss never respawns, the win screen is only reachable via the defeated signal, and _update_checkpoint is monotonic from 7 so the bad value persists through every re-save (e.g. pause-menu Save Now). All beacons light (id <= 7) while the party stands in the village — the claimed inconsistency is real too. Scope caveats match the claim: requires a tampered/foreign save (normal play caps checkpoint at 4), and only the run is locked — New Game escapes via reset_run(). The vector is not purely hypothetical: encounter_manager.gd:33-35 documents areas were already renumbered once WITHOUT a save-version bump; an id that no longer exists hits this path. The suggested clamp at load or in _apply_resume is a sound fix.

bugs.title: Unvalidated `level` string from a save is round-tripped into the single-quoted JS eval, so a quote/backslash in it silently kills all future saving on web
bugs.where: scripts/game.gd:200 (level accepted as any string), 209-210 (v3 migration immediately re-saves it), 154 (text embedded in single-quoted localStorage.setItem eval)
bugs.scenario: 1) Hand-edit the web save to `"level": "cove'x"` (or any value containing ' or \), keeping v:4 — or v:3, in which case _apply's migration re-save fires immediately. 2) _apply stores the raw string into current_level_id (LevelRegistry.get_def falls back to the valley for world-building, but the id itself is never sanitized). 3) Every subsequent Game.save() builds `localStorage.setItem('norfolk_path', '{..."level":"cove'x"...}')` — the embedded quote terminates the JS string literal, eval throws a SyntaxError that Godot swallows, and the setItem never runs. 4) Saving stays broken (with the UI claiming success, see the silent-save bug) until the player crosses a level transition, which overwrites the id with a clean registry value.
bugs.impact: All persistence silently dead while the bad id is live; the appearance fields are normalized against the catalog but `level` is the one saved string with no such guard, contradicting the file-header comment that promises no stored string can contain a quote or backslash. Hand-edit-only and web-only, hence low severity.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced end-to-end in scripts/game.gd. Line 200 accepts the save's "level" as any raw string (str(parsed.get("level","valley"))) with no registry/catalog validation — unlike appearances, which are whitelisted via AppearanceCatalog.normalized (appearance_catalog.gd:54-72). Line 148 writes it back into every save, and line 154 embeds JSON.stringify output in a single-quoted JS string for JavaScriptBridge.eval; Godot's stringify does not escape single quotes (JSON has no ' escape), so a level like cove'x terminates the JS literal, eval throws SyntaxError, Godot's web eval shim catches/logs it and returns null, and setItem never runs. last_saved is set on line 152 BEFORE the eval, so the UI still reports a successful save. Lines 209-210 make a hand-edited v:3 save fire the broken re-save immediately on Continue. Recovery via level_transition.gd:69 (clean exported target_level) and reset_run (game.gd:121) is confirmed; no in-game path writes a dirty id, so this is hand-edit-only and web-only, matching the claim's low-severity scoping. The header comment (game.gd:9-11) promising no stored string contains a quote/backslash is genuinely unenforced for the level field. Minor imprecision only: a backslash in the id does not break the eval immediately — it survives one round trip and instead corrupts the stored JSON so a later load rejects it (save effectively lost) — a different failure mode than the quote's instant SyntaxError, but still breakage. Core mechanism confirmed exactly as described for the quote case.

bugs.title: Boss fight becomes permanently unstartable (and Irene free-killable) after any door round-trip at checkpoint 4
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:188-199 and 216-222; E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\boss_irene.gd:62-66
bugs.scenario: Walk east until checkpoint 4 registers (boss activates normally). Instead of fighting, step onto the library door mat 9 tiles north of her (valley cells Rect2i(161,17,3,1), level_registry.gd:72) or any other door/edge, then walk back out. LevelTransition._travel sets Game.resume_requested=false and reloads main.tscn. On reload, _apply_resume returns at line 188 (not a resume), and _update_checkpoint can never call _activate_area again because activation is nested inside 'if reached > Game.checkpoint' (line 216) and checkpoint is already 4 and monotonic. The freshly spawned boss stays _active=false forever: she stands still (boss_irene.gd:62-66 zeroes velocity and returns), never throws books, shows no HP bar, but still takes damage (Enemy.take_damage has no _active gate, enemy.gd:90). Sword her 9 times (36 HP / 4 dmg) with zero opposition; _die still sets Game.boss_defeated, saves, and rolls the win screen.
bugs.impact: Whole boss fight skippable with a trivial door round-trip, or (for a player who doesn't attack a passive NPC-looking boss) the fight appears broken and can never be started in that scene instance; only a full wipe or a save Continue restores activation.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully verified by static trace; every cited line checks out. (1) LevelTransition._travel (level_transition.gd:73-74) sets Game.resume_requested=false and reloads main.tscn on EVERY door/edge crossing; Game is an autoload so checkpoint=4 survives in memory. (2) On reload, setup() spawns a fresh boss with _active=false, then _apply_resume returns at encounter_manager.gd:188-189 (not a resume, boss_defeated false), skipping the checkpoint>=BOSS_ID activation at 198-199. (3) _update_checkpoint (205-222) can never re-activate: reached starts at Game.checkpoint (already 4, the max area id), so 'reached > Game.checkpoint' (line 216) is permanently false; the only other _activate_area caller is _rearm_area:301, which requires a full-party wipe — matching the claim's only in-scene recovery paths (wipe or save Continue). (4) Inactive boss: boss_irene.gd:62-66 zeroes velocity and returns — no books, no HP bar, and also no contact damage (her _physics_process overrides Enemy's, the sole caller of _try_contact_damage). take_damage is inherited from enemy.gd:90 with no _active gate and no override. Numbers verified: max_hp=36 (boss_irene.tscn), attack_damage=4 (player.gd:85), SwordHitbox mask 4 matches her layer 4 — exactly 9 free hits; _die (boss_irene.gd:143-150) unconditionally sets Game.boss_defeated, saves, and emits defeated → win screen. (5) Premise verified: library mat Rect2i(161,17,3,1) at level_registry.gd:72 sits 9 rows north of the boss row (island_map.gd rows 17/26), east of area-4's center x (cell 156), so checkpoint 4 registers before the mat is reachable; _travel refuses only on win_pending/fade, so mid-fight escape works. Even if that door were somehow impassable, any of the valley's 9 transitions (e.g. the open-road cove edge Rect2i(190,24,2,5)) reproduces the same state, so the bug does not hinge on library-door reachability. Progression-breaking (boss skip with zero opposition, or apparent softlock), not cosmetic. Caveat: verified by code trace, not a live playtest.

bugs.title: Inactive boss can be sniped dead with arrows from west of her activation line, skipping the entire fight
bugs.where:
E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\enemy.gd:90-98 (no _active gate in take_damage); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:148-150, 205-222; E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\player.gd:91-92; E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\arrow_projectile.gd:8-10
bugs.scenario: Boss spawns inactive at cell (162,26) = world (2600,424); she only activates when a player's x crosses the area-4 center x = 2504 (cell 156*16+8). Stand at x ~2500 on the road (checkpoint stays 3, boss never activates), face east, and fire full-draw arrows: range = 200 px/s * 1.2 s lifetime = 240 px >= the 100 px gap, and the arrow mask (4) ignores terrain anyway. The inactive boss takes every hit (take_damage has no activation check), zeroes her own knockback, never throws a book, and dies after ~12 arrows. _die fires the defeat line, saves boss_defeated, and triggers the win screen.
bugs.impact: Complete risk-free boss skip: no books, no phase 2, no HP bar, win screen granted. Also desyncs the save (boss_defeated=true at checkpoint 3).
bugs.verifier_note: Verified line-by-line. Enemy.take_damage (enemy.gd:90-98) gates only on _dead; BossIrene inherits it without override, so her _active flag (boss_irene.gd:33) never blocks damage — it only disables her own AI. The boss is instantiated inactive at load (encounter_manager.gd:60-61, 148-150) at cell (162,26) = world (2600,424) (TILE_SIZE=16, cell_center=cell*16+8, map_data.gd:32-33), and activate() fires only when a live player's x >= area-4 center x = 2504 (_update_checkpoint, encounter_manager.gd:205-222) or on resume/rearm with checkpoint >= 4. A player parked at x~2500 (west half of the checkpoint's own walkable road cell, x span 2496-2512) keeps checkpoint 3 and the boss inert. Full-draw arrow: speed 200 (player.gd:92, set at player.gd:310), lifetime 1.2s (arrow_projectile.gd:10) = 240 px range from spawn at ~x2510; boss 14x20 collider spans x[2593,2607], y[414,434], arrow at y~418 with r=4 overlaps — hit connects. Arrow collision_mask=4 matches only the boss layer (arrow_projectile.tscn:11, boss_irene.tscn:20), so terrain never blocks the shot. Damage = round(lerp(1,3,1.0)) = 3; max_hp = 36 (boss_irene.tscn:23) = exactly 12 arrows (~20 s). Kill path: _die -> BossIrene._die (boss_irene.gd:143-154) sets Game.boss_defeated=true, saves (with checkpoint still 3 — the claimed save desync is real, though _apply_resume at encounter_manager.gd:186-187 tolerates it), emits defeated -> _on_boss_defeated -> win screen after 3 s. Inactive she zeroes velocity every frame and never throws or shows the HP bar, so the snipe is risk-free (aside from area-3 stragglers unrelated to the boss). Minor nits only: gap to collider edge is ~93 px not 100, and the kill is exactly 12 arrows. Verified by static trace of every link; not live-playtested. Real, complete, gameplay-breaking boss skip.

bugs.title: Wipe-respawn only rearms the checkpoint's own area — cross-area pursuers camp the respawn point
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:231-239 and 286-309
bugs.scenario: Village slimes (area 0) have aggro_radius 0 = chase forever from level load (enemy.gd:29-34, enemy_slime.tscn has no aggro_radius override). Skip them and run east; they follow. Die at the area-1 or area-2 fight with the slimes in tow: _on_downed rearms only _area_by_id(Game.checkpoint), so the checkpoint area's survivors return_home(), but the area-0 slimes (and any woken skeletons/bowmen dragged past a later checkpoint line, since the aggro latch at enemy.gd:157-162 is permanent) are untouched. All players respawn_at(area.center) — exactly where they died — with the pursuers standing on the spot. They re-engage immediately after the 0.6 s spawn invulnerability; with two 2-damage camp skeletons camping (contact_cooldown 1.0, alternating), a surrounded 6-HP player can be re-downed in ~2.4 s, repeating the cycle.
bugs.impact: Respawn camping up to a repeat-death loop; directly contradicts the documented rearm contract ('survivors just reset to their post').
bugs.verifier_note:
Code trace confirms every load-bearing mechanic of the claim.

1) Rearm scope: encounter_manager.gd:231-239 — `_on_downed` computes `_area_by_id(Game.checkpoint)`, respawns all players at that area's center, and calls `_rearm_area(area)` on that single area only. `_rearm_area` (286-309) iterates only `area.instances`; grep across scripts shows no other caller of `return_home` on a wipe. Enemies from any other area are untouched.

2) Slimes chase from load, forever: enemy_slime.tscn sets no `aggro_radius`, so the script default 0.0 applies (enemy.gd:34), and `_awake` (enemy.gd:157-162) returns true unconditionally when `aggro_radius <= 0`. `_nearest_player` has no range cap and there is no leash/return logic outside `return_home`. (Minor: slime speed is 26 in the scene, not the script-default 30 — cosmetic.)

3) Aggro latch is permanent: `_aggro = true` at enemy.gd:161 is cleared only by `return_home()` (enemy.gd:80-88), which only the checkpoint-area rearm invokes. RangedEnemy (bowman/mage) inherits the same latch. So camp skeletons/bowmen dragged past a later checkpoint keep chasing across wipes.

4) Respawn exposure: player.gd `respawn_at` (350-360) gives full HP and `_invuln_time = INVULN_AFTER_HURT = 0.6` (player.gd:70) — the claimed 0.6 s. Skeleton scene: contact_damage 2, contact_cooldown 1.0, move_speed 34, aggro_radius 110; player max_hp 6, max_speed 60. Two adjacent skeletons re-down a passive player in roughly 2.0-2.4 s (hits gated by the 0.6 s post-hurt invuln at ~0.6/1.2/~1.8-2.2), matching the claim's arithmetic. Note `_try_contact_damage` (enemy.gd:180-187) burns the attacker's cooldown even on invuln-absorbed hits, so exact timing jitters slightly.

Two accuracy caveats that do not change the verdict: (a) "respawn exactly where they died" overstates — respawn is the checkpoint road spot (area.center), typically west of the fight; pursuers idle at the death spot while no player is targetable, then converge at 26-34 px/s (worst realistic case: camp skeletons past the area-3 checkpoint, ~96 px away, seconds from the spot; if the pursuers themselves downed the player near the checkpoint they are literally on it). (b) It is respawn camping but not a guaranteed softlock — the player revives at full HP, outruns (60 vs 34) and outdamages (sword 4 vs skeleton HP 5) the pursuers, so "up to a repeat-death loop" applies to passive/surrounded players.

The design intent is provably violated: the header contract (encounter_manager.gd:15-18, "survivors just reset to their post and idle again") and especially the camp-checkpoint comment (102-104, checkpoint placed west of the camp's aggro edge "so a wipe respawns you on the road, not inside the clearing") are defeated when latched cross-area pursuers follow the player — they end up standing on that road spot. Real gameplay bug, not cosmetic.

(4) The awaited timer on EncounterManager (never freed) still fires Game.change_scene(WIN_SCENE) at 3.0 s — no fade in flight to block it — cutting to the win screen mid-resurrection. bugs.title: Enemy bolts ignore terrain and shooters need no line of sight — players get hit through solid walls/trees

bugs.title: Corner steering can oscillate forever in concave corners (remaining hole in the round-3 fix)
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\enemy.gd:141-152
bugs.scenario: _maybe_steer only ever picks the collision tangent whose dot with to_target is positive. In a concave pocket (inside corner of a building/fence L, e.g. the farm fence rectangles at rows 41-45 or 66-73) with the player standing diagonally beyond the corner vertex, BOTH walls' player-positive tangents point deeper into the pocket: the enemy commits 0.35 s into wall B, expires, re-aims into the corner, is blocked, commits along wall A back into the pocket, and repeats indefinitely — it can never choose a tangent that moves away from the player to escape. Additionally, during knockback the 'progress < move_speed * delta * 0.3' test at line 145 can trip while knockback opposes motion against any wall, causing spurious 0.35 s steer commits.
bugs.impact: Chasers visibly jitter/oscillate stuck in concave geometry while the player stands safe on the other side; enemy is trivially killable with arrows from there.
bugs.verifier_note: Confirmed by full code and level-data trace (not a live playtest). The tangent pick at scripts/enemy.gd:149-150 guarantees the committed steer direction always has non-negative dot with to_target; in a concave inside corner with the player standing diagonally beyond the vertex, every escape direction has negative dot and is therefore structurally unselectable, while both selectable tangents point head-on into the perpendicular wall. Resulting loop: re-aim into corner (wedged, progress~0 trips line 145) -> 0.35s commit pushing head-on into the other wall (zero motion; slide projection cancels, and default wall_min_slide_angle 15 deg hard-stops head-on velocity) -> expire -> repeat forever. One mechanical detail in the claim is off — the commits produce zero motion rather than movement "deeper into the pocket" — but the essential defect (no escape direction can ever be chosen; permanent visible jitter/livelock while the player holds position) is exactly what the code does. Supporting facts verified: enemies are CharacterBody2D motion_mode=1, circle r=7, collision_mask=1 (scenes/enemy_slime.tscn); fences spawn per-cell StaticBody2D rects on default layer 1 (scripts/main.gd:373-403), so enemies collide with them; the cited fence pens exist in scripts/island_map.gd lines 41-45 and 66-73, including reachable inside corners (66-73 L-junction pocket open to the SW; cottage-A pen at lines 47-48 open at the bottom). Arrow knockback (enemy.gd:94) pushes away from the shooter — deeper into the corner — so the stuck enemy is indeed free kills. The secondary claim (knockback against a wall while chasing trips the line-145 progress test and causes a spurious 0.35s steer commit) is also real (90 px/s knockback vs 26-30 px/s chase speed, needs a wall collision that frame) but minor/cosmetic. Mitigations that bound severity: player movement can flip a tangent sign and free the enemy, and return_home() resets steer state on checkpoint retry; the script comment explicitly accepts dumb, non-pathfinding steering — but the claim frames it as the remaining hole in the round-3 fix, which is accurate.

bugs.title: Key remaps silently lost on web if the tab is closed soon after rebinding (user:// IndexedDB flush is asynchronous)
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\controls.gd:116 (_save writes user://controls.json via FileAccess; also reset() at controls.gd:74-77)
bugs.scenario: In the web build, open Pause > Controls, rebind any key (each rebind calls Controls._save(), which writes user://controls.json through FileAccess), then close the browser tab within the next moment. On the web platform user:// is an Emscripten IDBFS: FileAccess.close() only writes to the in-memory FS, and the engine persists it to IndexedDB via an asynchronous, deferred FS.syncfs kicked from the main-loop iteration (OS_Web schedules the sync; the IndexedDB transaction itself completes asynchronously). Closing the tab before that completes discards the write. Next session Game._ready > Controls.apply_saved() finds the old file (or none) and the remap has reverted. Same for Reset to Defaults: the DirAccess.remove_absolute deletion can fail to persist, resurrecting the supposedly-cleared overrides. Note the contrast: game.gd:153-154 deliberately routes the run save through synchronous localStorage for exactly this reason, but controls.gd did not get the same treatment.
bugs.impact: Player rebinds controls, closes the tab, and next visit the bindings are back to defaults (or a cleared reset comes back) with no error - looks like the settings screen is broken. Run progress is unaffected (localStorage path).
bugs.verifier_note: Verified end-to-end. controls.gd:_save (lines 106-119) writes user://controls.json via FileAccess unconditionally — no web branch — and is called on every rebind (controls_menu.gd:104 -> rebind() line 70); reset() (lines 74-77) deletes via DirAccess.remove_absolute, also unconditional. game.gd:69 (Controls.apply_saved in the autoload _ready) re-reads whatever persisted, so a lost sync silently reverts bindings. The claimed contrast is real and deliberate: game.gd:153-154 routes the run save through synchronous localStorage on web, with the file header citing synchronous persistence as the reason, while controls.gd:9 acknowledges "(IndexedDB on web)" yet takes no countermeasure. No mitigation exists (Web export preset with empty custom_html_shell; no beforeunload/syncfs handling anywhere). The engine mechanism is documented and acknowledged by the repo's own research: docs/roadmap.md:81-85 and 154 record that user:// on HTML5 is IndexedDB synced asynchronously via FS.syncfs on file close ("don't save in the instant before the tab closes"), citing Godot issue #39643 / PR #42266; a .dos session artifact repeats the lesson. The reset() case is understated in the claim — DirAccess deletion doesn't even fire the syncfs-on-close trigger, so the deletion persists only if a later write kicks a sync. Caveat: I could not execute a live timed tab-close race on a hosted export; the window is normally sub-second (one main-loop iteration + IndexedDB commit) but widens if the tab is backgrounded (rAF-throttled loop). Real silent data-loss bug, low stakes (bindings only; run save unaffected). Fix: mirror game.gd's web/localStorage branch for the tiny controls JSON.

bugs.title: Camera limits never recomputed on browser-window resize; interior rooms pin off-center with a one-sided void band
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\follow_camera.gd:80 (_apply_bounds uses get_viewport_rect().size, called only from _ready at line 50 and set_zoom_preset at line 68)
bugs.scenario: Stretch is canvas_items/expand with canvas_resize_policy=2, so the browser canvas resize changes the 2D viewport width continuously (e.g. 16:9 window -> viewport 640x360, 21:9 -> 853x360, 32:9 -> 1280x360). _apply_bounds() samples get_viewport_rect().size exactly once per level load (deferred from _ready) or on a zoom-preset change - there is no connection to viewport size_changed. Interiors are smaller than the view (cottage_map.gd is 13x10 tiles = 208x160 px vs a 256x144 view at Normal zoom 2.5), so _axis_limits() bakes a view-sized centred window into limit_left/right/top/bottom. Trigger: enter the cottage in a normal-width browser window, then maximize onto an ultrawide monitor / press F11 / rotate a tablet. The view is now wider than the frozen limit span, Camera2D cannot satisfy both limits and pins to one edge (left/top-priority clamping), so the room renders shoved to one side with the entire excess void on the other side. Shrinking the window instead leaves the limits wider than needed, letting the camera drift past the room edge as the player walks. State persists until the next level change or zoom cycle.
bugs.impact: After any mid-session browser resize, fullscreen toggle, or device rotation inside an interior, the room sits visibly off-center against an asymmetric black/void band (or the camera slides off the room while walking). Self-heals only on scene change or zoom cycling.
bugs.verifier_note: Verified end-to-end by tracing code and configs. follow_camera.gd:80 samples get_viewport_rect().size inside _apply_bounds(), which is called only from _ready (deferred, line 50) and set_zoom_preset (line 68); project-wide grep confirms no viewport size_changed connection or other recompute path exists. project.godot sets stretch canvas_items + aspect expand (base 640x360), so the 2D viewport size tracks window aspect continuously, and export_presets.cfg:39 sets html/canvas_resize_policy=2 (adaptive browser canvas). Cottage interior is exactly 13x10 tiles (cottage_map.gd) at 16px tiles (tileset.tres:89) = 208x160 px vs a 256x144 view at default zoom 2.5, so _axis_limits (lines 89-93) bakes a view-sized centred limit window on the x-axis using the load-time viewport width. After a mid-session widen (16:9 -> ultrawide/F11), the new view width (~341px at 2.37:1) exceeds the frozen 256px limit span; Camera2D cannot satisfy both limits and pins to one edge, rendering the room off-center with a one-sided void band (~24px vs ~109px). Shrinking instead leaves stale-wide limits that let the camera drift across the small room as the player walks. Self-heal claims also verified: level changes are full scene reloads via change_scene_to_file (game.gd:101, re-running _ready), and zoom cycling calls set_zoom_preset (pause_menu.gd:163-165). Only unverified detail is the exact engine clamp-edge priority (left/top vs right/bottom) - either way the one-sided pin follows. Severity: real but visual-only - no gameplay/state impact, recovers on any door transition or zoom cycle; fix is trivial (connect viewport size_changed to _apply_bounds).

bugs.title: Pause menu shows "Saved HH:MM" even when the web save write failed (storage blocked / quota)
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:152 (last_saved set unconditionally before the localStorage write at line 154; surfaced by pause_menu.gd:169-171)
bugs.scenario: Game.save() stamps last_saved BEFORE attempting the write. On web the write is JavaScriptBridge.eval("localStorage.setItem(...)"): if the browser blocks storage (Chrome's 'block all cookies/site data' setting makes localStorage access throw SecurityError; quota exhaustion throws QuotaExceededError), the exception is caught inside Godot's godot_js_eval wrapper and merely logged to the console - GDScript sees no failure. The player presses Save Now in the pause menu, gets the green 'Saved 12:34' confirmation, closes the tab, and the run was never persisted (has_save() returns false next visit, so no Continue option). The desktop branch has the same shape: if FileAccess.open returns null the timestamp is still shown.
bugs.impact: False save confirmation: a player in a storage-restricted browser profile is told their run is saved when nothing was written, and loses the run on tab close. Checkpoint autosaves (encounter_manager.gd:218) fail equally silently, so the whole session is unknowingly ephemeral.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced fully. game.gd:152 stamps last_saved BEFORE the write; web write (line 154) is JavaScriptBridge.eval("localStorage.setItem(...)") with the return value discarded and no read-back — Godot's godot_js_eval wraps the eval in try/catch and only logs exceptions (GodotRuntime.error), so SecurityError (Chrome block-all-cookies makes the localStorage accessor throw) or QuotaExceededError never reaches GDScript. save() returns void, so failure is undetectable by any caller on either branch; the desktop branch (156-159) checks FileAccess.open for null but only skips the write — the timestamp is already set. pause_menu.gd:169-171 then unconditionally shows "Saved HH:MM", and encounter_manager.gd:216-220 unconditionally shows the checkpoint-saved HUD toast after autosave. Result: a storage-blocked browser session gets false save confirmations all session and has_save() returns false next visit (no Continue). Requires an environmental precondition (blocked storage / sandboxed embed / quota), but the code path has provably zero error detection, exactly as claimed. Fix direction: have save() verify (read back the key, or eval a try/catch returning a success flag / use localStorage.getItem comparison), return bool, and only stamp last_saved / show confirmation on success.

bugs.title: Rebind capture accepts a key with physical_keycode 0, leaving the action unpressable for the rest of the session
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\controls_menu.gd:104 (Controls.rebind(action, event.physical_keycode) with no zero guard; rebind at controls.gd:65-70 erases all existing events first)
bugs.scenario: During 'PRESS A KEY...' capture, any InputEventKey is accepted. On the web export, a key whose DOM code string Godot's dom_keys.inc does not map (browser-specific codes such as media/launcher keys, some international keys - the event still arrives with a usable logical key but physical_keycode KEY_NONE=0) passes the check at controls_menu.gd:99. rebind() then erases the action's bindings and installs a single InputEventKey with physical_keycode 0, which matches no physical key - e.g. P1 SWORD becomes dead immediately. Controls._save() persists {action: 0}; apply_saved() skips code <= 0 at next boot, so the action silently reverts to project defaults after a page reload - but within the current session the action stays dead unless the player finds Reset to Defaults. The Esc-cancel check at line 101 also compares physical_keycode, so such a key cannot even cancel the capture.
bugs.impact: A player who presses an exotic/unmapped key at the capture prompt gets that action (sword, roll, interact...) completely dead for the session with the menu showing '-' as the binding; the fix (Reset or reload) is not communicated anywhere.
bugs.verifier_note: Verified line by line. controls_menu.gd:98-105 accepts any pressed, non-echo InputEventKey during capture with no zero guard; a key arriving with physical_keycode 0 fails the Esc check (line 101 compares physical_keycode) and flows into Controls.rebind(action, 0) at line 104. rebind (controls.gd:65-70) erases ALL existing events for the action, then installs a single InputEventKey whose keycode/physical_keycode/key_label are all 0. Godot's InputEventKey::action_match falls through keycode->physical->key_label; an all-zero event matches key_label 0, which no normal key press carries, so the action (sword/roll/interact...) is dead for the rest of the session. _save() (controls.gd:111-114) falls back from physical NONE to keycode 0 and persists {action: 0}; apply_saved() (controls.gd:96) skips code <= 0, so after a page reload the action silently reverts to defaults — the damage is session-scoped. Trigger is real on the shipping platform: export_presets.cfg has only a Web preset, and Godot's platform/web dom_code2godot_scancode returns Key::NONE for any DOM code not in dom_keys.inc (browser-specific media/launcher keys, Fn, and notably mobile soft keyboards which send code:"" on every keystroke) while still delivering the event with a usable logical keycode. Two minor claim inaccuracies: the menu shows an empty string, not "-" (key_text returns "-" only when no InputEventKey exists; here one exists with code 0, so OS.get_keycode_string(0) -> ""), and RESET TO DEFAULTS is visible in the same menu, so recovery exists though it is never suggested as the remedy. Fix is a one-line guard: reject (or fall back to event.keycode) when physical_keycode == 0 in controls_menu.gd, and/or guard rebind() in controls.gd against code <= 0 to mirror apply_saved().

bugs.title: No compression or cache headers staged for the ~45MB web payload; delivery depends entirely on Vercel edge defaults
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\.github\workflows\deploy-web.yml:89-94 (Build Output API staging writes only {"version":3} - no headers/routes config, no precompressed .br/.gz artifacts)
bugs.scenario: The deploy stages the raw Godot export (index.wasm ~39MB + index.pck ~6MB) into .vercel/output/static with a bare v3 config.json: no headers section, no precompression step. First load on any connection: index.wasm is served as application/wasm, which Vercel's automatic edge compression treats as compressible (mime-db lists application/wasm compressible), so it likely ships brotli/gzip-compressed - but this is an undeclared reliance on default behavior, and dynamic edge compression of a 39MB body (vs a precompressed .br artifact) is both slower and unverified for payloads that large. index.pck is served as application/octet-stream, which is NOT in the compressible MIME set, so the pck ships at full size regardless (mostly-PNG contents limit the loss). Nothing in the repo pins or verifies any of this; a Vercel default change or a size cap on dynamic compression silently reverts first load to the full 39MB raw wasm.
bugs.impact: Worst case (wasm not compressed by the edge): ~45MB first-load transfer - about a minute on a 6Mbps connection staring at the Godot loading bar, and mobile players likely bounce. Best case it still forgoes the roughly 4-5x smaller precompressed-brotli path the workflow could stage. Load-time only; no gameplay or save corruption.
bugs.verifier_note:
The cited code fact is real but the scenario's central assertions are empirically wrong; what survives is a modest optimization/robustness nit, not the claimed 45MB risk.

VERIFIED TRUE: deploy-web.yml:89-96 stages the export into .vercel/output/static with a bare {"version":3} config.json — no headers/routes section, no precompressed .br/.gz artifacts (none exist in export/web/ either). Sizes match: index.wasm = 39,509,339 bytes, index.pck = 6,062,244 bytes. Delivery does rely entirely on Vercel edge defaults, and nothing in the repo pins or verifies that behavior.

EMPIRICALLY REFUTED (tested live against the latest CLI-prebuilt deployment norfolk-path-e9omp8jzo-chris-suryo-projects.vercel.app via a Vercel share link, since preview URLs are SSO-protected): (1) index.wasm is served Content-Encoding: br at 9,543,846 bytes (4.14x smaller; dynamic edge compression of the 39MB body worked fine, 1.8s on a cache MISS). (2) The pck sub-claim is flat wrong: index.pck IS served Content-Encoding: br at 1,650,395 bytes — mime-db lists application/octet-stream as compressible: true, the opposite of what the claim asserts. (3) Actual first-load transfer is ~11.3MB, not ~45MB (~15s at 6Mbps, not a minute). (4) The "4-5x smaller precompressed path forgone" is wrong: local brotli -q11 on the wasm yields 7,090,371 bytes, i.e. precompression would only beat the edge's dynamic br by ~26% (~2.4MB). Cache headers confirmed absent as claimed: Vercel serves max-age=0, must-revalidate, but repeat loads revalidate via ETag (304s) so the cost is round-trips, not re-downloads.

Remaining true substance: undeclared reliance on edge defaults, ~26% wasm savings left on the table, no immutable caching. Load-time-only, minor.

CRITICAL INCIDENTAL FINDING (worse than the claim): https://norfolk-path.vercel.app is currently serving 404 NOT_FOUND for every path including /. Cause: the deploy-web workflow FAILED on merge #19 (run 29549731823, 5s), while Vercel's Git integration independently auto-deployed the raw repo tree (no index.html at root) to production and took the alias. Production has been down since that merge. Fix requires both re-running deploy-web and ideally disconnecting the Git integration's auto-deploy (or ignoring the build) so it can't race the prebuilt deploy.

bugs.where:

orfolk-path\scripts\interactable.gd:48-53 (no is_fading gate on opening dialogue); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\dialogue_box.gd:37-48 (open() pauses the tree, sets Game.dialogue_active, no fade/win guard); E:\code

orfolk-path\scripts\island_map.gd line 47 ('L' talker cell at map (162,17)) + E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\level_registry.gd:72 (library door transition Rect2i(161,17,3,1) covers that same cell)

bugs.verifier_note: Every cited line and number checks out. interactable.gd:48-53 has no is_fading() gate and the tree is unpaused during a door fade, so E opens the DialogueBox mid-fade; open() (dialogue_box.gd:37-48) sets Game.dialogue_active=true and pauses the tree; the fade tween lives on the Game autoload which is PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS (game.gd:71, TWEEN_PAUSE_BOUND), so it completes while paused and change_scene_to_file frees the old scene with the DialogueBox still open — _close() (dialogue_box.gd:71-74), the ONLY code that resets dialogue_active (verified by grep), never runs, and SceneTree.paused persists across the swap. In the destination scene the only ALWAYS nodes are Game/pause_menu/controls_menu/dialogue_box; Esc dies at pause_menu.gd:97 on the stuck flag, the new box's input dies on _panel.visible, and all Interactables are both flag-blocked (interactable.gd:49) and pause-frozen — provably no recovery path, so it is a permanent freeze (refresh on web). Geometry recomputed: the sole 'L' is at map cell (162,17) (island_map.gd file line 47), main.gd:214-215 spawns the library_door Interactable there, and Rect2i(161,17,3,1) (level_registry.gd:72) covers that cell; the 18px talk radius > 16px tile means the E-TALK prompt is up from the from_library entry cell (162,18) onward, so the solo repro window (0.22s FADE_HALF fade-out after _travel) is real. bugs.title: Stale Game.current_entry survives Return-to-Title and hijacks the spawn point on a checkpoint-0 Continue
