orfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:43 (current_entry is runtime-only, cleared solely by reset_run at game.gd:122); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\level_transition.gd:70 (set on every door/edge use); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\player_select.gd:111-115 (Continue calls load_state but never clears current_entry); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\main.gd:229-232 (_entry_cell prefers the stale entry over the map's 'S'); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:188-191 (checkpoint <= 0 resume does not override the position)
bugs.scenario: New Game (reset_run clears current_entry). In the village, without ever crossing area 1's center (checkpoint stays 0), walk into the barn and back out - LevelTransition leaves Game.current_entry = 'from_barn'. Pause, Save Now (save: checkpoint 0, level 'valley'), then Return to Title (which deliberately does not call reset_run, so current_entry keeps its stale value). Pick Continue: load_state restores checkpoint 0 / level 'valley' but touches neither current_entry nor anything that would clear it. main._entry_cell() finds 'from_barn' in the valley's entries dict (level_registry.gd:53) and spawns both players at cell (43,18) in front of the barn instead of the map's 'S' spawn at (6,25); _apply_resume does not correct it because checkpoint is 0. The same mechanism fires for any stale door name present in the loaded level's entries dict (e.g. Continue onto a cove save after having entered the cove earlier in the session matches the cove's 'from_valley' entry).
bugs.impact: Continue drops the party at the last door used in the previous session segment rather than the spawn point - a visibly wrong resume position. Contained to safe locations (village door mats / cove arrival) because any save with checkpoint >= 1 teleports players to the checkpoint anyway, so it confuses rather than soft-locks.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully traced and reproduced statically; all cited lines and numbers check out. Game.current_entry is written only at level_transition.gd:70 and cleared only by reset_run (game.gd:122). A barn round-trip leaves current_entry="from_barn" (barn_int transition, level_registry.gd:167); walking to the barn (cell 43) never crosses area 1's center (cell 70), so checkpoint stays 0 — area 0 (id 0) can never advance the checkpoint since _update_checkpoint requires area.id > reached. Pause menu's Return to Title (pause_menu.gd:174-176) deliberately skips reset_run and touches nothing else; Continue (player_select.gd:111-115) calls load_state, whose _apply (game.gd:191-211) restores checkpoint/level/appearances but never current_entry. main._entry_cell (main.gd:229-232) then finds "from_barn" in the valley entries dict and spawns both players at (43,18) instead of the map's sole 'S' at (6,25) (verified by parsing island_map.gd with the strip_edges offset from map_data.gd:16). _apply_resume (encounter_manager.gd:188-191) returns early on checkpoint <= 0 and cannot correct it, while checkpoint >= 1 saves teleport to the checkpoint center (lines 196-197), masking the bug exactly as claimed. The behavior also contradicts the documented intent at game.gd:42-43 ("a Continue uses the level's own spawn/checkpoint"). Impact as stated: session-scoped (autoload resets on app restart), wrong-but-safe resume position (door mats / cove arrival), no soft-lock — a real, user-visible correctness bug of modest severity. Fix would be one line: clear Game.current_entry (or set it from nothing) in the Continue branch of player_select._confirm, or clear it in pause_menu._return_to_title.

bugs.title: Title menu stays live during the Continue fade: a stray Down+Enter runs New Game over the accepted Continue, then the next checkpoint autosave overwrites the real save with the downgraded run
bugs.where: scripts/player_select.gd:108-124 (_confirm has no Game.is_fading()/already-started guard; New Game branch calls reset_run after Continue was accepted); scripts/game.gd:94-100 (old scene stays interactive for FADE_HALF before the swap; only re-entrant change_scene is dropped); scripts/game.gd:118-124 (reset_run wipes the just-loaded checkpoint/boss state in memory); scripts/encounter_manager.gd:216-218 (later checkpoint advance calls Game.save(), persisting the wiped run over the old save)
bugs.scenario: Have a save at checkpoint 4 (2 players). On the title MODE stage press Enter (Continue): resume_requested=true, load_state() applies checkpoint 4, _go() starts the 0.22 s fade-out — but _stage stays MODE and _unhandled_input keeps running. Within the fade window press Down then Enter (a mash or a 'did it register?' second press): _confirm() takes the NEW GAME branch — resume_requested=false, Game.reset_run() zeroes checkpoint/boss_defeated/level in memory (a third Enter also runs set_player_count(1) on the 2P save). The scene then swaps: main/encounter_manager see a non-resume checkpoint-0 run — village spawn, every enemy alive, beacons dark. The save file still holds checkpoint 4 at this moment, but as soon as the confused player walks east past area 1's center, _update_checkpoint sets Game.checkpoint=1 and Game.save() overwrites the checkpoint-4/2P save with checkpoint-1 (player_count 1 in the third-Enter variant).
bugs.impact: Silent permanent progress loss in the single save slot: a late-game save is downgraded to an early checkpoint (and possibly to 1P) with no prompt and no way back; short of that, the Continue is silently converted into an unexplained fresh-village start with all enemies respawned.

Continue leaves _stage=MODE, so Down flips _selected (title Labels are unfocusable, so ui_down isn't eaten by GUI focus) and a second Enter runs the New Game branch: resume_requested=false + Game.reset_run() (game.gd:118-124) wipes checkpoint/boss_defeated/level/entry in memory (player_count untouched — the 2-press variant keeps 2P; a third Enter hits the COUNT branch with _selected reset to 1 and runs set_player_count(1), as claimed; the creator's accepted.emit needs row 8, so Enter-mash cannot reach the immediate save at player_select.gd:140). After the swap, main.gd reads current_level_id="valley" at instantiation, spawns at "S" (current_entry cleared), and _apply_resume (encounter_manager.gd:182-189) returns early on resume_requested=false with boss_defeated wiped — village start, all enemies alive, beacons dark. No save call exists in main startup, so the checkpoint-4 file survives until _update_checkpoint (encounter_manager.gd:205-218) fires on walking past area 1's center: Game.checkpoint=1, Game.save() overwrites the single slot (checkpoint 1, boss_defeated false, player_count 1 in the 3-press variant). Pause-menu Save Now (pause_menu.gd:170) persists it even sooner. Only narrowing nuance: the destructive path needs a directional press + confirm within the 0.22s window (or hover+click via mouse, which the IGNORE fade rect permits) — a pure Enter-mash is harmless. That makes it a mash/double-press race, but the mechanism is real, deterministic once triggered, and causes silent permanent loss of the only save.

bugs.title: Projectiles have no consumed-guard: one bolt/book damages both co-op players (and one arrow hits multiple stacked enemies) in the same physics tick
bugs.where: scripts/enemy_bolt.gd:32-35 (also scripts/book_projectile.gd:33-36, scripts/arrow_projectile.gd:32-35; stacking enablers: scripts/encounter_manager.gd:237-238, scenes/player.tscn:14-15)
bugs.scenario: All three projectile scripts do `take_damage(...)` then `queue_free()` inside `_on_body_entered` with no hit flag. In Godot, when two bodies first overlap an Area2D on the same physics tick, both `body_entered` emissions are delivered in the same flush; `queue_free()` in the first callback does not cancel the second, so the 'first target only' contract in each script's doc comment (enemy_bolt.gd:4-5, book_projectile.gd:4-6, arrow_projectile.gd:4-5) is violated. Concrete trigger (co-op): both players wipe, `_on_downed` respawns them at the identical pixel (`player.respawn_at(area.center)` for each, encounter_manager.gd:237-238), and players never collide with each other (player.tscn collision_mask = 1), so they stay/re-become superimposed in normal play; a single mage bolt (bolt_damage 2, radius 4 vs two overlapping radius-6 player bodies) arriving after the 0.6 s respawn invulnerability registers first-overlap with both bodies on one tick and both callbacks run take_damage — 4 total damage plus double knockback from one projectile. Player-favorable mirror: two 1-HP bats chasing the same player follow the identical steering law at identical speed and converge onto the same spot (enemies also don't collide with each other, mask 1); a single minimum-charge arrow (damage 1) whose first overlap covers both bodies kills both bats in one tick.
bugs.impact: Co-op players take double damage events from single enemy projectiles precisely in the situations where they are forced together (stacked wipe respawn, in-place revives, doorway fighting) — a 2-damage mage bolt becomes 4 of the party's 12 total HP in one frame. Conversely one arrow can cleave multiple converged enemies for free, silently breaking the single-target damage model in both directions.
bugs.verifier_note: Confirmed by code trace plus an empirical headless repro on the project's exact engine (Godot 4.7-stable). All three projectile scripts (enemy_bolt.gd:32-35, book_projectile.gd:33-36, arrow_projectile.gd:32-35) do take_damage() then queue_free() in _on_body_entered with no consumed flag, contradicting their own "first target it touches" doc comments. Repro mirroring the bolt (Area2D r=4 mask=2, two superimposed CharacterBody2D r=6 layer=2, queue_free() in the handler) fired BOTH body_entered callbacks in one flush — the second callback ran with area.is_queued_for_deletion()==true — proving one projectile damages two overlapping targets. Every cited enabler checks out: encounter_manager.gd:237-238 respawns all players at the identical area.center pixel; player.tscn mask=1 means players never collide with each other so they stay stacked; enemy scenes also mask=1 (enemies overlap freely); enemy_mage.tscn bolt_damage=2; player INVULN_AFTER_HURT=0.6 and max_hp=6 (party 12, so one bolt = 4/12 party HP); bat max_hp=1 and ranged_damage_min=1 for the arrow-cleaves-two-bats mirror. Per-instance guards (player i-frames, enemy _dead) do not protect the second target. Severity qualifier: the double-hit requires both targets to first-overlap on the SAME physics tick — a 90 px/s bolt moves ~1.5 px per 60 Hz tick, so targets must be near-exactly stacked along the travel axis (exactly what wipe-respawn produces) or side-by-side perpendicular to the shot; targets offset by more than one tick's travel are safe because the bolt frees before their entry. Real contract violation with a reachable but narrow trigger. Fix is the standard one-line guard: early-return if a _consumed flag is set (or is_queued_for_deletion()) before applying damage, in all three scripts.

bugs.title: Co-op: death is instantly voided with a free full heal when the killer is beyond 130px or blew itself up (bombschroom / long-range bolts)
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:242-253 (also encounter_manager.gd:41, 88-89, 271-279; scripts/player.gd:136-144 and 339-345 (DOWN set synchronously in take_damage); scripts/bomb_enemy.gd:69-80 (_explode removes itself from 'enemies' before dealing damage); scripts/enemy_bolt.gd:8-10 + scenes/enemy_bowman.tscn:17-19 / enemy_mage.tscn:17-19 (bolt range 300-360px >> REVIVE_RADIUS 130))

Let P2, at 3 HP or less, walk into its 22px detonate radius while P1 stands anywhere alive. Removes the entire death penalty for the bombschroom and for kited shooters.
bugs.verifier_note: Statically verified end-to-end; every gate in the claimed path holds. encounter_manager.gd:242-253 revives all downed players in place at full HP the first _process frame where any player is up and _enemies_near_players(130) is false — there is no timer, minimum down-time, or dependence on the downed signal anywhere in the codebase (grepped). player.gd take_damage sets State.DOWN synchronously (136-144, 339-345), so is_targetable() flips false within the killer's own damage call; the downed signal needs 0.7s in DOWN (player.gd:258-260) and never fires before the same/next-frame respawn_at (full 6 HP + 0.6s invuln, collapse cancelled). bomb_enemy.gd:71 does remove_from_group("enemies") BEFORE dealing its 3 damage (enemy_bombschroom.tscn contact_damage=3), so the killer is invisible to the revive check the instant it kills. Distances recomputed at TILE_SIZE=16: bomb (117,33)=(1880,536) to nearest area-3 enemy (135,25) is about 315px > 130, area-1 bowman about 351px — the deterministic camp scenario passes. Bolt ranges confirmed: bowman 100px/s and mage 120px/s x 3.0s lifetime = 300-360px >> 130px REVIVE_RADIUS, and players (speed 60) easily outrun shooters (28/22), so a trailing bolt kill with the live, permanently aggro-latched shooter beyond 130px triggers an instant mid-fight full-heal revive, violating the stated intent at encounter_manager.gd:39-41. Minor overstatements that do not change the verdict: in the bomb-as-last-enemy case the fight genuinely is over, so the bug there is the zero-cost same-frame death void rather than a bypass of the clear rule; and a standing-off mage (keep_distance 110 < 130) blocks revive, so the organic ranged trigger requires a fleeing/kiting kill. Real gameplay-affecting bug, not cosmetic.

bugs.title: Downed players can still open NPC dialogue, pausing the whole game mid-combat (no state gate on Interactable)
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\interactable.gd:48-53 (no is_targetable()/state check before _box.open) with scripts/dialogue_box.gd:37-48 (open() pauses the tree) and scripts/player.gd:132-133, 254-260 (DOWN player keeps processing input handlers; body stays in the talk radius)
bugs.scenario: 2-player game in the village: P2 is downed by slimes within 18px of a villager/Evan/Ariana talker and waits for the clear-revive. While DOWN, P2 presses E: Interactable._unhandled_input only checks _near > 0 and Game.dialogue_active, so the DialogueBox opens and get_tree().paused = true freezes P1's fight (players, enemies, EncounterManager revive logic all stop; only the dialogue advances). P2 can re-open it endlessly, stalling the fight indefinitely. Solo variant: a player in the 0.7s death collapse next to a talker opens a conversation as a corpse, deferring the wipe-respawn for as long as the box stays open.
bugs.impact: A dead/downed player retains a game-freezing ability: co-op fights can be stalled arbitrarily by the downed teammate, and a mid-collapse solo player can talk and postpone their own respawn. No permanent corruption (closing the box unpauses), but it is wrong state gating a player trivially hits near any talker.
bugs.verifier_note: Traced end-to-end and every link holds. interactable.gd:48-53 gates only on _near>0, _box!=null, and Game.dialogue_active — no player-state check — and line 51 accepts either p1_interact or p2_interact regardless of who is near. A DOWN player's body stays on collision layer 2 (player.tscn:14; _enter_down at player.gd:339-345 never touches collision), which is the Interactable's mask (interactable.gd:23), so _near stays >=1 and a downed player's interact press opens the box. DialogueBox.open() (dialogue_box.gd:47-48) sets get_tree().paused=true; the only PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS nodes in the repo are DialogueBox, the Game autoload, and pause/controls menus, so players, enemies, and EncounterManager (including _update_coop_revive, encounter_manager.gd:242-253) all freeze — the co-op clear-revive cannot progress while the box is open, and re-opening after each close stalls the fight to slivers between presses. Solo variant also confirmed: downed.emit() fires only when _state_time>=0.7 inside pausable _physics_process (player.gd:254-260), so opening dialogue mid-collapse defers the wipe-respawn indefinitely. Geometry is realistic: villager "N" at valley cell (46,30) sits 2 cells from the area-0 checkpoint with slimes at (50,28)/(55,28) (main.gd:210-211 spawns a talker there). Minor framing nit only: this is shared-keyboard couch co-op, so the stall is self-inflicted at one keyboard, not remote griefing; no permanent corruption (closing unpauses). Fix is the one-line gate the claim implies: require the pressing/near player to be targetable before _box.open.

bugs.title: Continue on a save written outside the valley (Save Now in the cove or an interior) resumes the checkpoint with zero valley bookkeeping - the first party wipe teleports everyone across the map, and at checkpoint 4 spawns a fresh ACTIVATED Irene on top of the respawn point
bugs.where:
E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:196,200 (checkpoint and level restored independently); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:56-58 (setup early-returns on non-valley levels, so resume_requested is never consumed) and 188-199 (_apply_resume prior-clear/activate only runs on a valley load); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\level_transition.gd:71-73 (walking back into the valley sets resume_requested=false, so the resume bookkeeping is skipped forever); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\encounter_manager.gd:236-239 (_on_downed respawns at _area_by_id(Game.checkpoint).center) and 286-302 (_rearm_area boss branch re-spawns AND activates Irene)
bugs.scenario: 1) Reach checkpoint 4 in the valley (autosave fires). 2) Run past Irene into the library door at (161,17) - or east to the cove edge - both reachable mid-fight. 3) Esc -> Save Now: the save is now {checkpoint:4, level:"library"} (pause_menu.gd:170; Save Now is available on every level). 4) Refresh, Continue: main loads the library; EncounterManager.setup early-returns (no areas), so resume_requested stays true but does nothing. 5) Step on the exit mat back to the valley: level_transition.gd:73 clears resume_requested, so _apply_resume returns at line 188 - no prior-area clear, no position override. The valley rebuilds with EVERY enemy alive again (areas' cleared flags are runtime-only; a direct valley Continue would have cleared areas 0-3 via lines 193-195). 6) Die to anything - even the area-0 village slimes right where you'd walk. _on_downed teleports both players to area 4's center (156,26), and _rearm_area(4) queue-frees the inert Irene, re-spawns her, and ACTIVATES her 96px from the respawn point. Same desync at lower checkpoints: a cottage save at checkpoint 2 respawns wiped players at the camp checkpoint on a fully repopulated map.
bugs.impact: Save/load desyncs checkpoint from world state: after continuing an out-of-valley save, all previously-defeated enemies are back, and any wipe warps the party to the late checkpoint - at checkpoint 4 that means a death to a village slime drops you into an instant activated boss fight, skipping areas 0-3 entirely (or, from the player's view, an unavoidable boss ambush at half-remembered progress).
bugs.verifier_note: Every cited line checks out. game.gd:196/200 restore checkpoint and level independently; pause_menu.gd:170 (Game.save in _save_now) is ungated and PauseMenu ships in main.tscn on every level, so Save Now in the library/cove writes {checkpoint:4, level:"library"}. On Continue, main.gd:199 passes the library's encounters id "" to EncounterManager.setup, _build_areas (92-95) empties _areas, setup early-returns at 57-58, and resume_requested is never consumed. Walking the exit mat back sets resume_requested=false (level_transition.gd:73), so _apply_resume bails at 188-189 — the prior-area clear (193-195) and position override are skipped forever, while runtime-only cleared flags mean all 5 areas respawn fully populated with Game.checkpoint still 4. A wipe then hits _on_downed (236-239): respawn_at(_area_by_id(4).center) = cell (156,26) = (2504,424), and _rearm_area's boss branch (289-301) frees the inert Irene, respawns her at (162,26) = (2600,424), and activates her — recomputed distance is exactly 96px. The library door Rect2i(161,17,3,1) and cove edge are reachable mid-fight (LevelTransition only refuses during win_pending/fade). Lower-checkpoint variant (cottage save at checkpoint 2 → wipe warps to camp center on a repopulated map) traces identically. One addition: the bug is broader than claimed — a plain door round-trip at checkpoint 4 with no save/refresh reproduces the same desync, since the transition clears resume_requested and the rebuilt valley repopulates all areas.

bugs.title: Implicit New Game (title opens straight on the 1P/2P stage when has_save() is false) never calls reset_run - a mid-session Return to Title with unreadable storage starts the 'new' run with the old run's checkpoint, boss flag, and level
bugs.where:
E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\player_select.gd:42-43 (_ready skips the MODE stage when has_save() is false), 122-130 (COUNT branch sets count/creator only - reset_run is called ONLY in the explicit NEW GAME branch at line 119); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\pause_menu.gd:174-176 (Return to Title deliberately keeps run state); E:\code
orfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:179-182 (blocked/failing localStorage.getItem -> null -> "" -> has_save false)
bugs.scenario: Web build with localStorage blocked (Chrome 'block site data', enterprise policy, or storage quota errors - the same environment as the known swallowed-save findings): 1) Player starts a game (title showed COUNT since nothing is readable), plays to checkpoint 3, maybe enters a cottage. 2) Esc -> Return to Title: Game keeps checkpoint=3, current_level_id="cottage", boss_defeated, current_entry (only the MODE->NEW GAME branch would reset them). 3) Title _ready: has_save() is false again -> jumps straight to '1 PLAYER / 2 PLAYERS'. 4) Player picks a count and creates a character, believing this is a fresh game. 5) The run starts inside the cottage (current_level_id never reset), with checkpoint 3 live: all beacons up to 3 pre-lit (_spawn_beacons area.id <= Game.checkpoint), and the first wipe teleports the party to the camp checkpoint deep in the map; if the previous run had won, boss_defeated=true silently deletes Irene from the new run.
bugs.impact: A 'New Game' that is not new: wrong starting level, pre-advanced checkpoint governing respawns, and a possibly pre-cleared boss - on any web client whose storage is blocked (exactly the clients that can never show a Continue option, so every restart funnels through this path).
bugs.verifier_note: Traced end to end. player_select.gd:22,42-43: _stage defaults to COUNT and only has_save() promotes it to MODE, so with unreadable storage the title opens on 1P/2P. reset_run() has exactly two call sites in the repo (player_select.gd:119 inside the explicit MODE->NEW GAME branch, and win_screen.gd:11); the COUNT branch (122-130) only sets count/resume_requested/creator. pause_menu.gd:174-176 deliberately keeps run state (its header even says "KEEPING the checkpoint (no reset_run)"), relying on the NEW GAME branch this path bypasses; Game is an autoload so checkpoint/boss_defeated/current_level_id/current_entry survive. game.gd:177-188: blocked localStorage makes JavaScriptBridge.eval return null (Godot catches the JS exception) -> "" -> has_save() false, and all autosaves fail silently, so the state stays dirty in memory only. Downstream is unconditional on resume_requested: main.gd:156-157/230-231 load the level and spawn from stale current_level_id/current_entry (new run starts inside the cottage at the old door), encounter_manager.gd:73 pre-lights beacons with area.id <= Game.checkpoint, and :231-239 teleports a wiped party to the stale checkpoint's road spot past uncleared areas. Two accuracy caveats: (1) the boss_defeated leg is overstated - win_screen.gd resets the run on accept, so a stale boss_defeated=true only survives via a Return-to-Title racing the WIN_DELAY window; (2) the bug requires web with unreadable storage (with working storage has_save() is true and the MODE reset is reachable); desktop is unaffected. Core finding stands: on any blocked-storage web client, every mid-session Return to Title yields a "new game" with the old run's level, entry, and checkpoint - functional, not cosmetic. Fix is trivial: call Game.reset_run() when entering COUNT implicitly (or reset in the COUNT confirm branch).

bugs.title: Desktop save is truncate-in-place with no temp-file swap: a crash or power cut during Game.save() leaves a truncated save.json, and the next boot silently discards the whole run (no Continue offered, no message) before overwriting it
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:156-159 (FileAccess.open(SAVE_PATH, FileAccess.WRITE) truncates immediately, then store_string - no write-to-temp+rename, no backup copy); same pattern for key remaps at E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\controls.gd:116-119; consumed by has_save()/_apply at game.gd:168-174,191-194 which treat unparseable JSON as 'no save at all'
bugs.scenario: Desktop build: a checkpoint autosave (encounter_manager.gd:218) or Save Now fires; FileAccess.WRITE truncates save.json to zero bytes; the process dies before/while store_string flushes (OS crash, power cut, force-kill of the window). Next launch: _read_raw returns the truncated text, JSON.parse_string yields null, has_save() returns false - the title jumps straight to the player-count stage with no hint a save existed. Accepting the character creator then calls Game.save() (player_select.gd:140), overwriting the corrupt file with a checkpoint-0 save, destroying any chance of manual recovery.
bugs.impact: Total, silent loss of the run from an interrupted write: the player is never told a save existed or got corrupted, and the recovery window closes the moment they start a new game. Same non-atomic pattern loses all key remaps.
bugs.verifier_note: All cited lines verified. game.gd:156-159 opens save.json with FileAccess.WRITE (truncate-on-open per Godot docs; Godot's temp+rename safe-save is editor-only, never enabled in exported games) and there is no temp file, rename, backup, or fsync anywhere in the project (grep confirmed). A crash/power cut between open and close leaves a zero-byte or partial save.json. On next boot, has_save() (game.gd:168-174) parses the truncated text, gets null, returns false; player_select.gd:42-43 then skips the Continue/New Game stage entirely — no Continue offered, no message. Accepting the character creator calls Game.save() (player_select.gd:140), overwriting the corrupt file with a checkpoint-0 save, eliminating manual recovery. Autosave at encounter_manager.gd:218 makes the write window recur throughout play. controls.gd:116-119 has the identical non-atomic pattern and apply_saved() silently drops a corrupt remap file. The failure requires an interruption landing in a small (ms-scale per write) window, so real-world frequency is low, but the mechanism and total-silent-loss consequence are exactly as claimed. Fix: write to save.json.tmp, close, then DirAccess.rename_absolute over the target (both files), and optionally surface a 'save was corrupted' notice instead of treating unparseable JSON as no-save.

bugs.title: _apply is not conversion-safe: a wrong-typed field in a hand-edited save (e.g. "boss_defeated": "false" as a string) aborts _apply mid-assignment, leaving a half-applied run state that Continue then uses - verified empirically on Godot 4.7-stable
bugs.where: E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\game.gd:196-198 (checkpoint and player_count are assigned before bool(parsed.get("boss_defeated", false)) at line 198 can raise 'Invalid call. Nonexistent bool constructor' on a String value, aborting _apply with level/appearances never applied and the v3 migration at 209-210 never run); E:\code\norfolk-path\scripts\player_select.gd:114 (Continue ignores load_state()'s return, so the half-load proceeds silently)
bugs.scenario: The project's own documented fast-travel workflow injects saves by hand (memory: edit %APPDATA%\Godot\app_userdata\norfolk-path\save.json or localStorage['norfolk_path']). A natural hand-edit mistake - quoting a bool ({"v":4, "checkpoint":4, "player_count":2, "boss_defeated":"false", "level":"cove", ...}) or writing checkpoint/player_count as an object - passes has_save() (v parses fine), so Continue appears. load_state -> _apply sets checkpoint=4 and player_count=2 (lines 196-197), then line 198's bool("false") raises a script error that aborts _apply (proven in a headless 4.7 probe: bool(String)/int(Dictionary)/int(Array) all raise 'Nonexistent constructor' and abort the enclosing function at that statement). current_level_id stays "valley", appearances stay defaults, and the save file is never migrated/rewritten. The player Continues into the valley at checkpoint 4 with the wrong level and default characters instead of either a clean load or a clean rejection.
bugs.impact: Hand-edited or externally-generated saves produce a silent half-load (checkpoint applied, level/appearances/boss flag dropped) instead of failing loud; combined with the ignored return at player_select.gd:114 there is no signal anything went wrong. Low player exposure (requires editing the save), but the project's own playtest tooling does exactly this.
bugs.verifier_note: Reproduced end-to-end. (1) Code trace of game.gd:_apply (lines 191-211) matches the claim exactly: checkpoint (196) and player_count (197) are assigned before bool(parsed.get("boss_defeated", false)) at 198; level (200), appearances (204-208), and the v3-migration save() (209-210) come after. (2) Headless probe on the very binary the project's playtest driver uses (Godot v4.7.stable.official.5b4e0cb0f) with {"v":4,"checkpoint":4,"player_count":2,"boss_defeated":"false","level":"cove"}: bool(String) raises 'Invalid call. Nonexistent bool constructor.' and aborts the function at that statement — final state checkpoint=4, player_count=2, level still "valley", appearances/migration never applied, process keeps running (silent half-load, not a crash). int(Dictionary) likewise raises. (3) has_save() (game.gd:168-174) only checks Dictionary-ness and v in {3,4}, so the malformed save still offers Continue. (4) player_select.gd:114 discards load_state()'s return and calls _go() unconditionally; the probe additionally showed the aborted _apply returns null, which load_state()'s typed bool return silently coerces to false — so no signal exists even in the return path. (5) main.gd:156-157 builds the level from Game.current_level_id, so the half-load demonstrably resumes the wrong level with checkpoint applied. (6) The hand-edited-save workflow the scenario depends on is the project's own documented fast-travel technique (memory: godot-native-playtest-driver.md). Real correctness bug with honest low player exposure: requires an externally edited/generated save, but the project's tooling does exactly that. Fix direction: type-check fields (e.g. `is bool`/typeof) or validate before assigning, and have player_select surface a failed load instead of ignoring the return.

bugs.title: Boss Irene never deals contact damage — her ContactHitbox and contact_damage are dead, so players can hug her with near impunity
bugs.where: scripts/boss_irene.gd:62-95 (also scenes/boss_irene.tscn:25 contact_damage=1, :38-43 ContactHitbox; scripts/enemy.gd:134, scripts/ranged_enemy.gd:50 show the base/ranged paths that do call _try_contact_damage)
bugs.scenario: 1) Reach the library and activate the boss fight. 2) Walk straight into Irene and stand flush against/inside her (player mask 1 and enemy layer 4 don't collide, so full overlap is possible). 3) She stops at keep_distance 44 and never advances, and BossIrene._physics_process is a full override of Enemy._physics_process that never calls _try_contact_damage() and never decays _contact_cd — unlike Enemy (enemy.gd:134) and RangedEnemy (ranged_enemy.gd:50). Her scene explicitly configures contact_damage = 1 and a live monitoring ContactHitbox (radius 12, mask 2 = players), but no code ever reads it. 4) The only point-blank threat left is a book spawning at her (0,-8) offset every 1.7s, which takes ~0.06s to reach a hugging player — roughly half the intended point-blank damage pressure and no touch punishment at all between throws.
bugs.impact: Melee-hugging the boss is far safer than the scene data intends: touching her costs nothing, every other enemy in the game punishes contact, and the fight's main melee risk collapses to dodging one slow book every 1.7s at point blank. Combined with sword swings every 0.4s (4 dmg vs 36 HP), a player who face-tanks trades 9 swings against at most ~2 book hits.
bugs.verifier_note: Fully verified. BossIrene._physics_process (scripts/boss_irene.gd:62-95) completely replaces Enemy._physics_process and never calls _try_contact_damage() nor decays _contact_cd, while Enemy (enemy.gd:134) and RangedEnemy (ranged_enemy.gd:50) both do. The boss scene (scenes/boss_irene.tscn) sets contact_damage=1 (line 25) and ships a monitoring ContactHitbox (lines 38-43, radius 12, mask 2 = players) with no signal connections — grep confirms nothing else reads it, so both are dead data. Collision layers check out: player layer 2/mask 1 vs boss layer 4/mask 1 means neither collides with the other, so full overlap is possible, and inside keep_distance 44 she zeroes velocity. Numbers recompute: 36 HP / 4 dmg per 0.4s swing = 9 swings in ~3.6s vs books every 1.7s (75 px/s, ~0.06-0.15s to reach a hugging player) = at most ~2-3 hits, further cut by post-hurt i-frames. Every other enemy in the game punishes contact (including bombschroom via bomb_enemy.gd:76), and the boss script's doc comment claims to override only movement and death — the scene data documents intent the code silently drops. Real gameplay defect, not cosmetic. Fix is one line each: decay _contact_cd and call _try_contact_damage() in BossIrene._physics_process after move_and_slide().
